2603.29497.title: Distilling Human-Aligned Privacy Sensitivity Assessment from Large Language Models
2603.29497.paper_summary: Accurate privacy evaluation of textual data remains a critical challenge in privacy-preserving natural language processing. Recent work has shown that large language models (LLMs) can serve as reliable privacy evaluators, achieving strong agreement with human judgments; however, their computational cost and impracticality for processing sensitive data at scale limit real-world deployment. We address this gap by distilling the privacy assessment capabilities of Mistral Large 3 (675B) into lightweight encoder models with as few as 150M parameters. Leveraging a large-scale dataset of privacy-annotated texts spanning 10 diverse domains, we train efficient classifiers that preserve strong agreement with human annotations while dramatically reducing computational requirements. We validate our approach on human-annotated test data and demonstrate its practical utility as an evaluation metric for de-identification systems.
2603.29497.paper_summary_zh: 準確的文本數據隱私評估仍然是隱私保護自然語言處理中的一個關鍵挑戰。最近的研究表明，大型語言模型（LLMs）可以作為可靠的隱私評估者，與人類判斷達成強一致性；然而，它們的計算成本和在大規模處理敏感數據時的不切實際性限制了其在現實世界中的應用。我們通過將 Mistral Large 3（675B）的隱私評估能力提煉成僅有 150M 參數的輕量級編碼器模型來解決這一問題。利用涵蓋 10 個不同領域的大規模隱私標註文本數據集，我們訓練了高效的分類器，這些分類器在保持與人類標註強一致性的同時，顯著降低了計算需求。我們在人工標註的測試數據上驗證了我們的方法，並展示了其作為去識別系統評估指標的實用性。
2603.29497.author: Gabriel Loiseau et.al.
2603.29497.authors: Gabriel Loiseau, Damien Sileo, Damien Riquet, Maxime Meyer, Marc Tommasi
